summary: German author known for incorporating photographs into his prose. Works include The Rings of Saturn, Austerlitz, The Emigrants, and Vertigo. Wrote about the Holocaust, memory, and destruction in On the Natural History of Destruction.

works.name: The Rings of Saturn
works.description: Walking tour of Suffolk incorporating history, literature, and photographs.

clues.clue: Author encounters Michael Hamburger (translator of Paul Celan) during the narrative
examples: wrote about his encounter with Michael Hamburger in his The Rings of Saturn

works.name: Austerlitz
works.description: Novel about Jacques Austerlitz, who discovers his childhood identity as a Jewish refugee.

clues.clue: Lengthy novel about a man discovering his past
examples: This author's other novels include the lengthy Austerlitz

clues.clue: On the Natural History of Destruction discusses German evasion of the past; 'Air War and Literature'
examples: discussed the evasion of the past facilitated by before-and-after photographs

examples: A chapter of a novel is titled for this author who 'Takes the Waters at Riva'

comprehensive_summary:
W.G. Sebald (1944-2001) was a German author who lived in England and was famous for incorporating photographs into the text of his prose narratives. His works include The Rings of Saturn, Austerlitz, The Emigrants, and Vertigo.

The Rings of Saturn is a walking tour of Suffolk incorporating history, literature, and encounters with figures like the translator Michael Hamburger. Vertigo includes a chapter about Kafka titled 'Dr. K Takes the Waters at Riva.' On the Natural History of Destruction discusses how before-and-after photographs facilitated German evasion of the past about WWII bombing.

Author: On the Natural History of Destruction discusses German evasion of WWII bombing through before-and-after photographs
